top_findings.summary: The skill requests access to tools such as KillShell, NotebookEdit, TodoWrite, and Skill, which are unrelated to its primary purpose of connecting to REST APIs. KillShell allows terminating shell sessions, an action completely unnecessary for API integration. NotebookEdit and TodoWrite involve editing or writing files or data, contradicting the stated "read-only mode" and "cannot edit local files". Additionally, the ability to invoke other skills (via the Skill tool) could lead to privilege escalation or execution of unrelated actions. These permissions are excessive and not required for basic API connectivity tasks.
